summary: Implement deterministic RNG domains and authority randomness guard
acceptance_criteria: DeterministicRng exposes stable domain/substream construction from seed plus explicit domain keys such as system, day, entity, and purpose.

The selected PRNG algorithm, seed format, fork/domain semantics, and compatibility expectations are documented; if this is not fully covered by accepted ADRs, a new ADR is written before implementation.
Unrelated random calls in one domain do not perturb outcomes in another domain.
Authority logic cannot call Math.random; static guard and tests cover sim-core and other authority packages touched by M1.
RNG values, domain keys, and any persisted state are serializable through explicit versioned DTOs, ready for SIM-005 save integration.
Cross-environment tests prove the same seed/domain call sequence returns the same values in Node and Worker-capable builds.
No crypto RNG, real time, environment entropy, external RNG service, or new production dependency is used for authority randomness.
required_tests: Golden vector tests lock the PRNG output for documented seeds and domains.
Property tests prove domain isolation and stable replay for generated domain keys.
Static guard test fails on Math.random in authoritative simulation paths.
notes: This task may create a new ADR only if required to lock PRNG algorithm/compatibility semantics; it must not modify accepted ADR files.
Do not implement gameplay randomness beyond minimal deterministic test surfaces.
